summary: The bug is that LOGICAL-PATHNAME is interned as a symbol/type name but has no CLOS class, so find-class fails and any defmethod specialized on logical-pathname errors at load (blocking slynk-fancy-inspector). Fix: add a LOGICAL-PATHNAME bootstrap class as a subclass of the existing PATHNAME class in lib/clos.lisp. Lower-priority: bind the standard CL functions translate-logical-pathname and logical-pathname-translations (currently unbound reserved symbols).
risks: typep behavior must stay unchanged: builtins_type.c:233 returns 0 for LOGICAL-PATHNAME and must remain so (clamiga has no logical pathnames; a physical pathname must not test true as logical-pathname). The added class does not affect this because line 233 short-circuits before any CLOS dispatch.
Class ordering: logical-pathname must be defined AFTER pathname in clos.lisp so the superclass CPL exists when %compute-builtin-cpl runs.

ASDF compatibility: asdf.lisp defines its own translate-logical-pathname in the uiop package; binding CL:translate-logical-pathname is independent symbol identity, but tests/test_asdf.c and the trunk asdf load should be run to confirm no shadowing/collision regression.

Removing entries from cl_reserved_symbol_names is required by that file's own comment once the functions are backed, but must remove only TRANSLATE-LOGICAL-PATHNAME and LOGICAL-PATHNAME-TRANSLATIONS (keep LOGICAL-PATHNAME and LOAD-LOGICAL-PATHNAME-TRANSLATIONS).
Both host and Amiga tests are mandatory per CLAUDE.md; make test must pass before completion.
